scope: BearsCLOUD/bears-codex-workflow-plugin formal logic and orchestration roadmap

priority_policy.ordering_rule: execute prerequisites before consumers; do not start downstream write-scoped issues until blocking dependencies are complete or explicitly marked compatible
priority_policy.duplicate_policy: update existing issue instead of creating duplicate issue when objective is already covered

waves.intent: Clear accidental or blocking repository hygiene before formal chain execution.
Install the formal type/relation foundation that every downstream decision depends on.
Make decisions question/proof-driven instead of prompt-driven.
done_when: principles/goals are mapped to semantic types

waves.intent: Derive workflow permissions and enforce formal transitions.
done_when: candidate facts cannot derive write permission
state-machine invariants block execution when evidence is missing

waves.intent: Build machine-derived repository semantics and file context sources.
done_when: Python AST/code property facts are extracted
stale extracted facts cannot unlock execution

waves.intent: Compile minimal prompts from proof context and gate executor/model/tool choices.
done_when: OpenCode adapter is optional and deny-by-default

waves.intent: Enable zero-context goal execution, file-scoped fanout, and safe session reuse.
done_when: session reuse never carries raw chat context
autostart starts roadmap leaf nodes, not arbitrary issues

waves.intent: Block unsafe execution and closeout through invariants and GitOps degradation state.
done_when: orchestrator invariant check blocks unsafe execution
solved-but-open issues block closeout until reconciled

waves.intent: Use traces and analytics to improve roles, contexts, validators, and workflow rules.
done_when: degradation event triggers role/context remediation plan
process mining creates bounded improvement candidates

waves.intent: Reconcile old issues and safely onboard other repositories after formal control plane is available.
done_when: old backlog has proof-backed close/keep decisions
non-plugin repos have read-only or write-scoped onboarding proof

waves.intent: Use the formal orchestrator foundation to plan seller.besrs.ru GitLab to GitHub migration and shared platform integration.
done_when: seller migration roadmap uses semantic graph, formal decisions, file context, and platform-service reuse contracts

executor_guidance.allowed_parallelism_after_formal_foundat: parallelize independent extraction/catalog/profile issues only when write scopes are disjoint
manual_review_required_when: dependency issue is missing or closed without proof
issue mutates non-plugin repository without write-scope proof

doctor_expectations: validate this priority file against formal-logic contract
